title: Garlicky Cinnamon Greek Shrimp Skillet
description: A vibrant Mediterranean skillet featuring succulent shrimp tossed in a savory garlic sauce with a warm, unexpected hint of cinnamon. This energizing dish is finished with a balsamic reduction and fresh herbs for a sophisticated flavor profile.
instructions: Pat the shrimp completely dry with paper towels and season lightly with salt and pepper.
In a small saucepan over medium heat, simmer the balsamic vinegar for 5-7 minutes until it reduces by half and becomes syrupy; set aside.
Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the minced garlic and red pepper flakes to the skillet, sautéing for 30 seconds until fragrant but not browned.
Add the shrimp in a single layer and sprinkle the ground cinnamon evenly over them.
Cook the shrimp for 2 minutes per side until they turn pink and opaque, being careful not to overcook.
Stir in the lemon juice and half of the fresh parsley, tossing to coat the shrimp in the pan juices.
Remove from heat and drizzle the balsamic reduction over the skillet.
Top with crumbled feta cheese and the remaining parsley before serving immediately.
